calendar.matters:
APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Affirmed
MINI MINUTES: Appellant in Pro Per, Ranko Ostojic and Counsel for Respondent, Jansher Rasa, both appeared. Matters argued and submitted.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Banjamin Metthias, Esq., NELSON & KENNARD, (916) 920 - 2295, for Plaintiff Bank of America,
Ranko Ostojic, 415-517-7233, Prop Per Defendnat (In Person Appearance)

Defendant did not properly contest the court's tentative ruling. All parties are present.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT RANKO OSTOJIC MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

Defendant's motion to vacate default judgment and quash service of summons is denied. This court denied a same-titled motion by an October 4, 2021 order. The renewed motion filed January 3, 2022 fails to show new or different facts, circumstances or law and a valid reason for not presenting them with the first motion. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Quash Summons
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ranko Ostojic ( 415-517-7233) Defendant, in propria persona.
No other appearances.

The court notes there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule.
DISCUSSED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RANKO OSTOJIC'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Quash Summons:

Defendant's motion to vacate the default and default judgment is denied. Defendant fails to present any competent evidence or a declaration under penalty of perjury attesting to the facts that he asserts. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 2015.5.) The undisputed record shows proper service by a registered process server. (Proof of Service, filed May 20, 2021.) Defendant has not overcome the presumption of valid service arising from the return. (Evid. Code sec. 647.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
